sections.content:
Now I can see the initial state of Level 2:
- W(6,17) - white star at upper left area
- G(9,49) - gray object upper right
- O(12,24) - orange object upper middle
- G(21,8) - gray object upper left
- G(35,45) - gray object lower middle
- p(41,49) - purple object lower right
- G(48,54) - gray object lower right
So the initial layout has:
- Upper chain: G(21,8) - O(12,24) - G(9,49) connected to purple ring
- Lower chain: G(35,45) - p(41,49) - G(48,54) connected to orange ring
- W(6,17) - white star at upper left
Current state:
- G(12,24) - upper chain object (moved)
- O(17,29) - upper chain object (moved)
- G(18,57) - at purple ring
- G(21,8) - upper chain object (still at original position?)
- G(41,49) - lower chain object (moved)
- p(46,44) - lower chain object (moved)
- W(51,40) - at orange ring
Wait, I need to reconsider. Looking at the initial state:
- Upper chain: G(21,8) - O(12,24) - G(9,49) leading to purple ring
- Lower chain: G(35,45) - p(41,49) - G(48,54) leading to orange ring
Current state:
- Upper: G(12,24), O(17,29), G(18,57), G(21,8)
- Lower: G(41,49), p(46,44), W(51,40)
So W is at the orange ring. The upper chain objects seem to have shifted. The lower chain objects have also shifted.
I've been clicking objects from ring outward, which seems to move them. But clicking the ring again doesn't complete anything.
Let me try a different hypothesis: maybe I need to click the upper chain objects while W is at the orange ring, then click the purple ring.
Or maybe the mechanic is: click ring → W moves there → click objects from OUTWARD to ring (not ring to outward).
Let me try clicking the upper chain objects from outward to ring: G(21,8), O(17,29), G(12,24), then purple ring.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Initial state: W(6,17), upper chain G(21,8)-O(12,24)-G(9,49), lower chain G(35,45)-p(41,49)-G(48,54)
# Current: W at orange ring, upper chain objects shifted, lower chain objects shifted
# 
# New hypothesis: maybe I need to click objects from OUTWARD to ring (reverse of what I did)
# Upper chain outward to ring: G(21,8) -> O(17,29) -> G(12,24) -> purple ring
# But G(12,24) and O(17,29) positions have changed from original...
#
# Let me try clicking upper chain objects from far end to ring end
# Current upper chain: G(21,8), G(12,24), O(17,29), G(18,57 at ring)
# Try: G(21,8), O(17,29), G(12,24), then purple ring

action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 8},    # G farthest from ring
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 29},   # O middle
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 24},   # G closest to ring
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 57},   # purple ring
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
